text: When you write code, bugs are inevitable. The first step is identifying the bug—understanding what the program does wrong compared to what you expect. A systematic approach helps here: reproduce the error consistently, then isolate the part of the code causing it. Using debugging tools, like breakpoints in your IDE, lets you pause execution and inspect variables step by step. Another powerful strategy is logging—adding print statements or logging functions to track values over time. This is especially useful for issues that only appear in specific conditions. By combining these methods patiently, you can solve most problems efficiently and improve your code quality.
